name: glom (Pythonic Data Restructuring and Querying)

performance.notes: Pure Python; faster than complex dict comprehensions for nested data extraction

agent_readiness.notes: Data restructuring and deep access for nested Python data. from glom import glom, Coalesce, SKIP, T, Merge. data = {'user': {'profile': {'name': 'Alice'}}}. name = glom(data, 'user.profile.name'). Coalesce: glom(data, Coalesce('user.email', 'user.phone', default='')). List comprehension: glom(users, [('name', T.upper())]). Assign: glom(data, Assign('user.active', True)). T for method calls: T.upper(), T[0]. Merge for dict merging. Path access with defaults. Error messages show exactly where data is missing.

gotchas: Learning curve — spec syntax is unique and not immediately obvious
T object for method calls can be unintuitive for newcomers
Not a query language — no filtering (use list comprehensions or toolz.filter)
Overhead vs direct dict access for simple cases
best_when: Deep nested data extraction from API responses with excellent error messages
When KeyError/AttributeError messages need to show exact path of failure
Coalescing multiple possible key paths with fallbacks
summary: glom provides Pythonic deep access and restructuring for nested Python data. glom(data, 'user.profile.name'). Coalesce('email', 'phone', default=''). [('name', T.upper())] for list transformation. Assign('user.active', True). Excellent error messages show exact path of missing data. BSD-3 by Mahmoud Hashemi. Best for complex API response extraction with clear failure messages. Not for filtering — combine with itertools/toolz.
